title: agropyron repens subsp. littoreum
intro: Agropyron repens subsp. littoreum, also known as coastal couch or sea couch, is a subspecies of the common couch grass (Agropyron repens) that grows in coastal areas. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions such as respiratory issues, wounds, and skin irritations. The health benefits of Agropyron repens subsp. littoreum include its ability to soothe and calm the respiratory system, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. The herbal preparations of Agropyron repens subsp. littoreum include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions, although its use should be done under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
